user_query:
We’re a 55-person B2B SaaS startup in fintech (SMB spend management). Please run a comprehensive business process automation audit using the files under input/. Read: input/company_profile.json for company/industry/team size and any hourly_cost override; input/process_inventory.csv for our current processes and hours; input/tech_stack.txt for tools we already use; and input/policies.md for risk/compliance and change-management constraints. Then deliver three artifacts:

1) A structured audit report at output/audit/report.md following this outline exactly:
- Title line: "# Business Process Audit Report"
- Company header lines: "## Company: …", "## Industry: …", "## Team Size: …"
- "### Executive Summary" including:
  - "Total estimated hours wasted on manual work: X hrs/week"
  - "Potential annual savings: $X"
  - "Top 3 automation priorities (by ROI)" as a bulleted list of 3 processes (names must match those in roi.json)
- "### Process Analysis" with separate tables for all 8 categories using exactly these category labels and one table per category:
  - Communication & Email
  - Data Entry & Processing
  - Customer Operations
  - Document Management
  - Financial Operations
  - HR & People Ops
  - Sales & Marketing
  - IT & Security
  Each table must have columns (in this order): Process | Current State | Hours/Week | Automation Potential | Est. Savings | Priority
- "### Recommended Automation Roadmap" with three subsections using these exact phase labels and time horizons:
  - "#### Phase 1 (Week 1-2): Quick wins"
  - "#### Phase 2 (Month 1): Medium complexity"
  - "#### Phase 3 (Quarter 1): Complex workflows"
- "### ROI Summary" including the lines:
  - "Implementation cost estimate: $X"
  - "Monthly savings estimate: $X/mo"
  - "Payback period: X months"
  - "12-month net ROI: X%"
Use our inputs to tailor specifics and be concrete about the tools we already use. Where the inputs are missing, use standard assumptions from the skill brief.

2) A machine-readable analysis at output/audit/analysis.csv with these columns, in this exact order and header names: category, process, current_state, hours_per_week, automation_percentage, implementation_effort, est_savings, priority_score.
- category: one of the 8 exact labels above
- hours_per_week: numeric
- automation_percentage: decimal between 0.6 and 0.85 unless justified otherwise
- implementation_effort: integer 1–5 (1=low, 5=high)
- est_savings: weekly $ savings computed as hours_per_week * hourly_cost * automation_percentage
- priority_score: (hours_per_week * hourly_cost * automation_percentage) / implementation_effort
Use $36/hr fully-loaded cost by default, unless input/company_profile.json provides an hourly_cost override.

3) A ROI summary at output/audit/roi.json with these keys and numeric values (unless otherwise specified):
- average_hourly_cost_used
- total_manual_hours_per_week (sum of hours_per_week across all rows)
- annual_savings (sum of est_savings * 52)
- monthly_savings_estimate (sum of est_savings * 4.33)
- implementation_cost_estimate
- payback_period_months (implementation_cost_estimate / monthly_savings_estimate)
- twelve_month_net_roi_percent (((monthly_savings_estimate*12 - implementation_cost_estimate) / implementation_cost_estimate) * 100)
- top_priorities (array of exactly 3 process names in descending priority_score order)

Ensure internal consistency between report.md, analysis.csv, and roi.json: the top 3 priorities must match; savings and ROI math must align; categories must use the exact labels above; and assumptions must be stated if you override defaults. Write all files only under output/.
